mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects persistent consumer demand that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or positive return frequency in top quintile of RSAFS growth not exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending income contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of instances when UNRATE has risen > 0.3pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and a low federal funds rate create negative real yields that push investors out of fixed income and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; the effect depends on the joint level of both series and does not hold for either series in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms a univariate CPIAUCSL model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of restrictive monetary policy, falling industrial production signals an amplified negative growth shock that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 55% of instances when INDPRO declines while FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not statistically lower than the unconditional Sharpe (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances following two consecutive monthly declines in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20d return hit rate is below 55% when GDPC1 QoQ growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside a falling unemployment rate confirms a supply-demand virtuous cycle where output growth is absorbed by strong labor income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; either series alone fails to capture the full effect.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal does not outperform the best univariate proxy by at least 2 percentage points in forward return hit rate, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate over three consecutive months reduces present values of long-duration cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances following three consecutive monthly increases in FEDFUNDS.
